human_verification.verification_notes: Manual review confirmed the package stays specific to ED flow planning and allocation, preserves EMTALA and clinical-triage boundaries, escalates privacy and payer interpretation, and does not claim a retained implementation.

required_boundary_metadata: EMTALA medical screening and stabilizing treatment boundary
PHI, minimum necessary, sensitive-record, and audit boundary
payer authorization, eligibility, medical necessity, coding, and utilization boundary
staffing, diversion, room closure, budget, and command approval boundary
tenant_runtime_dependencies: EHR, ED board, ADT, bed-management, staffing, transfer, diagnostic, consult, authorization, privacy, and incident retrieval hooks
tenant flow playbook, split-flow rules, fast-track rules, boarding thresholds, ambulance offload thresholds, LWBS thresholds, tie-break rules, escalation matrix, and RACI
state law, privacy, consent, sensitive-record, behavioral-health, transfer, and security procedures
payer, plan, authorization, eligibility, referral, utilization, coding, and post-ED placement rules
refusal_or_orchestrator_return_condition: missing tenant facts or source records would change the recommendation
clinical, EMTALA, privacy, payer, staffing, command, legal, safety-critical, or approval authority is required
sources, systems, payer rules, state law, command instructions, or tenant policies conflict materially
PHI disclosure basis, recipient authority, or minimum necessary scope is unclear
requested action would delay emergency screening or stabilization

risk_review.residual_risk: Tenant-specific law, local policy, source-system latency, staffing rules, and payer contracts must be retrieved at runtime before definitive allocation recommendations.
